narrative: OMX30 opens into a firmly bullish technical environment with the index 8.5% above its 200-day moving average and ADX 58.9 signaling strong directional conviction. Global equity markets delivered broad-based strength overnight (S&P +0.79%, NASDAQ +1.52%, DAX +0.28%), supporting a positive correlation bias of +0.275%. However, RSI at 74.16 and Stochastic K/D both in overbought territory (89.5/87.9) flag mean reversion risk and warrant caution on fresh long entries. USD/SEK strength (+0.34%) provides a structural tailwind for Swedish exporters, though falling US 10Y yields (-46bp to 4.37%) create mixed signals: positive for equities on lower discount rates but compressive for bank NIM expansion. The index sits 0.8% below all-time highs with resistance at 3180 (50-MA) and support at 3085 (lower Bollinger band).
morningBrief: OMX30 primed for a bullish open on broad global equity strength and USD/SEK appreciation, though technicals scream caution on overbought conditions. RSI at 74 and Stochastic K at 89.5 signal mean reversion risk—expect profit-taking on strength. Support holds at 3085 (lower Bollinger band), resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Key trade: industrials and exporters (Volvo B, Atlas Copco A, Ericsson B) benefit from strong USD/SEK, but watch copper weakness (-1.21%) pressure Boliden. Banks face headwinds despite resilience as yields compress; SEB A and Handelsbanken A vulnerable if rate-cut expectations intensify. VIX uptick to 16.94 (+2.98%) suggests underlying caution—stay defensive on dips. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is primary calendar risk.
drivers: Global equity strength: S&P 500 +0.79%, NASDAQ +1.52%, DAX +0.28% provide positive regional correlation signal
OMX30 in strong bull regime: +8.5% above 200-MA, +2.6% above 50-MA, ADX 58.9 (strong trend)
USD/SEK strength at +0.34%: tailwind for major exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson)
US yields down -46bp: reduces refinancing pressure and supports equity valuations

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK at +0.34% and global industrial PMI momentum from DAX/S&P strength support export-heavy machinery and equipment players.
US 10Y yield down -46bp to 4.37% compresses NIM expansion expectations; resilience supported by broader equity strength but duration risk dominates.
NASDAQ +1.52% and positive momentum from US equities offset DCF compression concerns from lower yields. USD/SEK strength also benefits Ericsson export revenues.
Copper -1.21% and gold -0.85% override positive commodity backdrop; copper weakness particularly damaging for Boliden's revenue mix.
Defensive characteristics supported by VIX uptick and yield compression; no sector-specific catalysts but relative safety appeals amid overbought technicals.
US 10Y yield down -46bp reduces refinancing cost pressures; yield compression supports real estate valuations relative to alternatives.

watchList.reason: USD/SEK +0.34% tailwind, industrials momentum, export-heavy exposure. Watch for mean reversion pullback to 3050 area as entry on overbought technicals.
Exporter beneficiary of USD/SEK strength and DAX/industrial momentum. Tight correlation with global manufacturing sentiment.
watchList.catalyst: US manufacturing PMI, continued USD strength

watchList.reason: NASDAQ +1.52% supports tech sentiment; USD/SEK +0.34% boosts export revenue. However, watch for overbought unwind.
Copper -1.21% and gold -0.85% create headwinds despite bull market backdrop. Commodity weakness is structural near-term risk.
watchList.catalyst: Copper price stabilization, Chinese demand data

watchList.reason: Yield compression (-46bp on US 10Y) pressures NIM expansion narrative. Risk/reward unfavorable despite equity market strength.
watchList.catalyst: Fed commentary, Swedish central bank meeting signals

watchList.reason: EUR/SEK +0.15% and weak EUR/USD (-0.19%) create sourcing cost headwinds. Watch for consumer rotation if overbought technicals unwind.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is primary data risk. No major Swedish economic releases today. Monitor Fed speakers for rate-cut signal clarification given yield compression.
topRisk: Overbought technicals (RSI 74.16, Stochastic 89.5/87.9) create mean reversion vulnerability; sharp pullback to 3085 support possible on profit-taking or negative data surprise. VIX +2.98% suggests underlying uncertainty despite equity strength. Trump-related trade policy headlines remain tail risk.
technicalSummary: MACD above signal line with histogram +4.95 (bullish)
ADX 58.9 (very strong trend, bullish DI bias intact)
52W positioning: 97% of range, only -0.8% from all-time high
